_status: BOOTSTRAP — NOT PRODUCTION-SIGNED. The expected_sha256_at_v0_3_cut placeholder values below are filled at the v0.3.0-rc1 release ceremony from Sigstore's upstream Fulcio/CTFE/Rekor keys; see _tuf_root/README.md. The C18 marker gate (tests/test_synthetic_c18_marker_gate.py) fails closed on these placeholders under REQUIRE_C18_HARDENING=1 until the ceremony runs.
role_separation_note: This file is a SEPARATE TUF role from the C18 release role per OQ-2 disposition. Distinct rotation cadence: NEXI re-pins on Sigstore key-rotation announcements; not coupled to C18 release cadence. Matches Sigstore's BYO-TUF convention (https://blog.sigstore.dev/sigstore-bring-your-own-stuf-with-tuf-40febfd2badd/).
comment_bootstrap_disposition: v0.3 bootstrap: the actual PEM bytes for Fulcio + CTFE + Rekor public keys live in Sigstore's upstream TUF root at https://tuf-repo-cdn.sigstore.dev/. NEXI's `sigstore-trust-root` TUF role mirrors those targets under the consumer-side trust dir; consumers fetch the PEM bytes by TUF protocol with consistent-snapshot + monotonic-version + expiry checks. The expected sha256 digests below are the CURRENT (2026-05-20) Sigstore production-good values; rotation triggers a re-pin via the ceremony procedure in `_tuf_root/README.md`.

fulcio.pub.purpose: Sigstore Fulcio root CA — issues short-lived X.509 certs for keyless signing; binds OIDC identity claims to ephemeral signing keys

ctfe.pub.purpose: Sigstore CTFE (Certificate Transparency Front-End) log signing key — Sigstore signs every Fulcio cert into the public CT log; this key verifies CT inclusion

rekor.pub.purpose: Sigstore Rekor transparency-log signing key — Rekor's signed tree heads are verified against this key; STH gossip (c18-019) uses this

sigstore_root_threshold.json.purpose: Rotation-ceremony policy doc for the sigstore-trust-root role itself

rotation_policy.comment: Per Pass-1 tightening item 14 sub-bullet 7: ≥2-of-3 distinct human approvers per rotation ceremony. NEXI's one-person-CSO + AI-workforce posture means hardware-rooted MULTI-SITE token holdings (maintainer-laptop + cloud KMS + cold-storage) satisfy the 'distinct' requirement structurally; humans-only interpretation of distinct is honestly deferred to v1.5+ per OQ-7 alongside SLSA L4.

honest_hedge: v0.3 ships the sigstore-trust-root role STRUCTURE — the separate role file, the target enumeration shape, the rotation-policy doc reference, the python-tuf integration in c18_tuf_client.py. The actual PEM bytes are pulled from Sigstore upstream at first-trust ceremony AND embedded into the OCI image via the c18-001 flake at release-tag-cut. Production sha256 digests above are filled by the ceremony tooling. Rotation discipline matches Sigstore's BYO-TUF convention.
